chunks.topic: Introduction to HTML Video, Audio, and Media
chunks.text: In today's video, we will learn how audio, video, and media elements are used in HTML. In our very first video, we saw that we can embed videos inside HTML. Today I will tell you how to autoplay a video, how to keep a video muted, and how you can enable and disable video controls. Along with that, we will also look very closely at audio, and how media can be displayed overall on a page. So definitely like this video and stay tuned because the examples I have used for video tags are very explosive. Let's go to the computer screen, let's roll the intro.

Friends, this time I have created a folder named video 10 and I will not waste your time by repeatedly shift-right-clicking and opening with code. In fact, I will create index.html. And today's video topic is video: how to play a video, how to insert a video into an HTML file, how to insert audio, how to insert media. So adding a video is very straightforward. I will copy this here, which is our video tag, it has an SRC attribute and it has some other attributes that I will discuss with you. But first, I want to tell you here that if you write video SRC equals this here, then if you have video.mp4, it will play. I have video.mp4, so let's see here, and I have put the controls here, and I will click here to preview it, and you can see here that the video has appeared, okay. Now this is a very large video, so what I can do is give it a width. So I can say here width is equal to 555 pixels, and as soon as I do 555 pixels. In fact, I don't need to write px, I'll just do 555, just like we don't need to write px in images, we won't need to write px here either. So as soon as I did width equals 555, in fact, I'll do one thing, I'll do height equals 555 so that it becomes even smaller. In fact, I'll make it even smaller, I'll make it 255. And you see it's here, okay. Now I'll play it because I wrote controls, I can play it. Look here, the road is better than America for now, what's my name? Bhupendra Jogi. Where have you been in the US? I've been to many places in the US. Tell me the name. Bhupendra Jogi. So, you can play videos like this. Now there are some attributes of the video tag here, see, using the video tag is very simple. You should have a video, you gave the path of the video, then you wrote controls, so these controls will appear for you. You can download it, full screen it, mute it, picture-in-picture it, your browser automatically provides all this. But let me tell you about some attributes, the first attribute is SRC, you must know that you put the video here, the video file name is SRC. This video file can also come from an external URL, do you understand? Meaning, if I have uploaded a video on Amazon S3 or somewhere else, I can put that URL here, meaning it's not necessary for the file to be on your computer, it can also be on your server, or come from a third party. Now what do controls do? Adds video controls like play, pause, and volume. Which we saw here in the Bhupendra Jogi video, that you can see the controls here. Now, the thing is that the browser automatically checks what controls will be added to your video and what won't. Then see what autoplay will do? It will automatically play as soon as the page loads, okay. So if I put autoplay here, let's say I put autoplay here, okay. Look here, you have the market roads, how are they doing? Tell me here. They are doing well. So you can see here that it has autoplayed, okay. So I will remove autoplay from here. And our next attribute is loop, meaning once our video has played, it will keep looping. Let me show you. Look here, you have Bhupendra Jogi. Look here, you have the market's. So it started again, once I finished it, it started again by itself, okay. So this loop works something like this. The next attribute is muted, muted mutes the video. So if I write muted here, sometimes we want the video to be muted. In fact, when I add videos here on codewithharry.com and I demonstrate something like downloading VS Code or something, I use the muted attribute because I don't want you to hear any background noise if there was any when I recorded the video. For example, I want to show you that this is an element, so I will scroll and make a video, highlight it like this, then I don't want audio in it, so I mute it. So you can use the muted attribute on your websites. Then poster, poster is very important. So if I have an image here, download.jpg, okay. If I write poster equals and I write download.jpg, then you will see it like this, okay, the poster looks like this, if I play it, the video will play. So it's like a thumbnail, okay, you've seen YouTube video thumbnails, it's exactly like that, this poster attribute. So all these attributes, you can use them, width and height, you know that they specify the dimensions of the video, as you saw I added both width and height here. So I hope you all understood this.

Now, after this, what I want to tell you is audio. So you have seen all the video attributes, it's very straightforward. I want you to create a nice website, which I will also give you at the end of this video in a quick quiz, so definitely listen to the quick quiz. In fact, I'll just put the heading 'Quick Quiz' right now, okay, so I don't forget. I will give this to you now, and this is going to be a very fun quiz, and everyone should definitely solve it. But even before that, as soon as you write audio, SRC appears here, and in SRC, I am putting sachin.mp3. So as soon as I put sachin.mp3, you see here, I will reload it and the audio didn't appear here. Which is strange, let me see what the problem is here. SRC equals I put this, I will also have to write controls here. So if I don't write controls, I won't see the audio. Look, the audio is here now, I can see the controls, I can also set its volume, so I'll play this audio. Sachin, what is in Sachin? He's a small Sachin. Okay, so this is our audio, if I play it muted, it won't play. And I can also fast forward it. He's a small Sachin. Okay, you've seen it, so this is an audio named sachin.mp3. Now what we will do is look at some attributes of this audio. SRC, you know, is the source of the video file, okay. Then after that, you know controls are for play, pause, and volume, which we just saw. What will autoplay do? It will automatically play it. If I put autoplay here, Sachin, what is in Sachin? It played automatically. He's a small Sachin. So what happened with autoplay, our audio automatically played. After this, the next attribute is loop, what will loop do? It will repeat it if I want to loop it. So I autoplayed it and I'll loop it. Sachin, what is in Sachin? He's a small Sachin. Once. Sachin, what is in Sachin? Second time. Small. So you saw it repeated, meaning it played once, then it played again because I had looped the audio, okay. So you saw this, I looped sachin.mp3, meaning it played once, then played again, then played again, okay, Sachin, what is in Sachin, okay, it kept repeating. Because this mp3 will play repeatedly. So there are many use cases for this, that when you want this mp3 to repeat, okay, so if you want your mp3 to repeat, you can use this. Now after this, what will muted do? It will mute the audio, and you definitely don't want your audio to be muted by default in most cases. But sometimes we will want the audio to be muted and we can tell the user here that whoever wants to listen, unmute it and listen. Sachin, what is. When do we do this? When there are multiple audios. If you want to show five-six audios at once, you can mute them, and your user can play and unmute them as they wish, okay. So the control is in your hands, you can use this if you want, okay. Overall, that's the point. So I hope you understood this, and enjoyed it. Now preload, the most confusing attribute. And that's why I have also explained it on codewithharry.com. That the preload attribute can have these values. The first value can be none, this is the default value, it indicates that the browser should not preload the audio at all, okay. Preload means if you want the browser to fetch the data beforehand, then you will use the preload attribute, otherwise you will not use the preload attribute, okay. Preload equals none is the default value. Preload equals metadata means that the browser will only preload the metadata, i.e., the information about that audio file, meaning it will load it beforehand. And doing auto will make the browser preload the entire audio, okay. So if I do preload equals auto, it will download and automatically load so that whenever the user plays it, the audio is saved, okay. So this is the function of the preload attribute. And this preload attribute is often asked in interviews, so you should know what the preload attribute is.

Now many people were asking if we can use Replit? See, I haven't used Replit in this course, but replit.com is an amazing website if you want to use HTML etc. on your phone, Replit also has an app for iOS, and for Android, so you can try it out. Many people are asking, how to code on a phone? Replit. But what I want right now is VS Code, which most people want to use, prefer, so I thought why not make videos in VS Code. But I personally use Replit quite a lot, in fact, videos also come in Replit.

chunks.topic: HTML SVG Tag and External SVG Files
chunks.text: The next topic is SVG, related to media. What is SVG? SVG is a format through which you can create images with very high graphics, vector images, so if you copy, you can basically create circles and all these things in it. So SVG is used in exactly the same way as our IMG tag is used. So look, if I put SVG here, this SVG will appear here, so look, this circular SVG appeared here. Video, audio, SVG, these were inline elements, that's why we are seeing them in a line, okay. You have seen it here. Now what's here is that you can also save the SVG file separately, you can save the SVG file separately. For example, if I write img.svg here and save the SVG content in it. So if I then write img here and write SRC img.svg, then you see here that I didn't see the image, if I write alt here and write my SVG image. And if I write my SVG image, then you will see here that my SVG image appeared like this, but I didn't see the SVG. Why did this happen? This happened because the SVG, I write SVG file in browser, and whenever you want the SVG file to appear in the browser, you will put a declaration above, I will show you. So I wrote SVG file not showing here, and there's a Stack Overflow answer here that will tell you. So you have to write this, I'll show you, XML like this, see, you have to add this, it's called a namespace declaration. So for this SVG, you have to add this thing. And now you can see the SVG here. So this thing is very important, and many people will definitely get stuck in this problem whenever you create your own websites in the future, I keep getting stuck, okay. So I'm telling you that the problems you will face, I have already told you, you haven't even encountered the problem yet, I have told you the problem that is coming and also given the solution.

The next topic is iframes. Iframes are also a very simple thing, that with iframes you can embed another website into your own website, okay. For example, if I want to embed this webpage, I can do it using an iframe like this. So look, I wrote iframe this, and I will copy this URL and paste it here, and you can see here that this entire website has been embedded here in this small iframe, okay. You would definitely want to embed it in a larger iframe, but if you want it to be embedded, you can embed it like this. I'll do one thing, I'll increase its height a bit. So width is equal to width is written, I'll set width equal to, let's say, 122, height equal to 444, okay, let's say. So look, it came out like this, okay. Now, definitely, it was fitting in this width, so it came in the same line, but if I increase the width a bit, let's say I make the width 322, okay, then look, my entire website is embedded here. Do you see? So you can embed a website. Another use case for iframe is YouTube video. If you want to share a YouTube video and click on embed here, it will give you the iframe code. And you can start it here in any way, if you want it to start at a particular time, you can do that here too. The point at which you start and share it, that time appears here, let me show you, wait. For example, let's say I want it to start from here, okay, let's say the video starts from here. So what I will do is, I will click on share, after clicking on share, I will click on embed, I will do start at 12:19 and copy it, see the copy button here. And I will put it here in my code. So I'll comment out this iframe and put the iframe below, okay. I'll word wrap it so you can see the code properly. So now what I will do is, after pasting this, I will show you the document, look, this video has been embedded here, okay. So I hope you understood this.
